levels.title: Picture at the gate
levels.scenarioDescription: Alex is excited about a big day and wants to share a quick selfie from outside a school. The wide frame keeps the building and entrance in view. It’s a personal milestone—think about who you’d want in the room for that kind of news, not just whoever scrolls by.

defaultPost.caption: First day at Riverside High — main entrance selfie!! So hyped :)

Original.title: Original
Original.description: Wide frame; school context and background visible.

Option A.title: Option A
Option A.subtitle: Blur
Option A.description: Blur the friends in the background

Option B.title: Option B
Option B.subtitle: Blur
Option B.description: Blur school sign and the night market sign

riskZonesOriginal.Option A: Readable fictional school sign: RIVERSIDE HIGH

goldPathSummary.captionCoachNote: Default caption names the school and the entrance—edit so nothing pins you to this place.

levels.title: Friends at the fair
levels.scenarioDescription: A group shot from a community fair—spring banners and booth details sit in the background. It’s a day-out with your people: the kind of memory that often lands in a smaller circle than a billboard.

defaultPost.caption: Spring Fest 2026 with the crew — we basically camped at the main stage all day!

imageAsset.alt: Group photo at a community fair with banners

riskZonesOriginal.Option A: Readable banner SPRING FEST 2026 and line MAIN STAGE

goldPathSummary.captionCoachNote: Default caption repeats the event name, year, and main stage—edit so the post doesn’t pin time and place. Keep a broad region tag on.

levels.title: New wheels
levels.scenarioDescription: A proud new-car photo—the plate and a background billboard are still in the frame. You’re celebrating something personal; worth matching who sees the post to who you’d tell about the new ride first.

imageAsset.alt: New car photo with plate and background visible

captionExamples: So happy with my new ride.
goldPathSummary.coachNote: Default caption is already fine—keeping it is OK. Edit only if you want a different tone.

levels.title: Sideline snap
levels.scenarioDescription: A sideline snap from practice—jersey details and a schedule board sit in the background. It’s team energy: the people who share that dugout matter more than a random scroll.

defaultPost.caption: 4:30 practice at Riverside Sports Complex — track was brutal. Santos brought it today!

imageAsset.alt: Sideline practice photo with board and jerseys

Original.title: Original
Original.description: Schedule board and jersey details visible.

Option B.title: Option B
Option B.description: Blur the jersey and name tag R. SANTOS

riskZonesOriginal.Option B: Jersey and name tag R. SANTOS

goldPathSummary.captionCoachNote: Default caption shouts the time block, venue, and a jersey name—edit to something generic.

levels.title: Coffee catch-up
levels.scenarioDescription: A relaxed coffee-table photo with friends—nothing in the frame needs hiding. Everyday warmth, the sort of moment people often reserve for folks they actually grab coffee with.

Original.title: Original
Original.description: No crop needed; gold path for this safe level.

Option A.title: Option A
Option A.description: Blur the banner in the background

goldPathSummary.coachNote: If players open image edits, A/B are decoys—focus on audience, broad location on, and caption.

levels.title: Cousins’ weekend
levels.scenarioDescription: A family group photo outdoors on a picnic hill—warm poses, open sky. A weekend-with-family vibe; some people keep that album closer to relatives and close friends than to the open feed.

levels.title: Airport gate
levels.scenarioDescription: Waiting at the gate with friends—screens and signage in the background, but nothing here is meant to read like a leak. Still a travel-day moment: who you’d text “we’re at the gate” is a useful gut check.

Option A.title: Option A
Option A.description: Blur the depature banner in the background

levels.title: Mirror checklist
levels.scenarioDescription: A mirror selfie while trying a new outfit—notes on the glass and bits around the mirror pack a lot of detail into one shot. A fit check at home; some people share that mainly with visitors who’ve already seen the room.

defaultPost.caption: New fit — mirror pic at my place in Riverside Township. Notes on the mirror are literally my whole week lol

locationTagDisplay.subtitle: Near home (neighborhood-level; pairs with in-photo address leak)

imageAsset.alt: Mirror selfie with notes in room

Option B.title: Option B
Option B.description: Blur the address and student id

riskZonesOriginal.Option B: Blur the address and student id

goldPathSummary.captionCoachNote: Default caption confirms neighborhood + that readable notes are yours—edit so nothing in text stacks on the photo leaks.

levels.title: Birthday at the door
levels.scenarioDescription: A birthday gathering near the door—balloons, smiles, and readable text on the wall by the entrance. Celebratory, but the scene still whispers “here’s home” louder than a hall-party snapshot.

defaultPost.caption: Best day ever!! Happy birthday to me 11/03/2026!

imageAsset.alt: Birthday gathering near a door with address visible

Original.title: Original
Original.description: Address text and table context visible.

goldPathSummary.captionCoachNote: Avoid confirming “here’s my house.” Keep a broad city tag on.

levels.title: Night market
levels.scenarioDescription: An evening street-food shot—a big sign in frame nails the place and hours. Late-night energy; the kind of night people sometimes recap in a tighter thread before they’d broadcast it wide.

defaultPost.caption: At Night Market Gate B — here til 11:30 close! Zone A signs everywhere, you can’t miss this spot.

Original.title: Original
Original.description: Sign with place and hours visible.

goldPathSummary.captionCoachNote: Default caption repeats gate, closing time, and zone text from the scene—edit so the words don’t double the signage. Broad region tag can stay on.
goldPathSummary.coachNote: captionLegacy is an older default line kept for reference only.
